label: Great Lakes average ice cover decline linked to climate change

comments.text: It’s been the coldest winter in 15 years for parts of the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic, with record ice cover on the Great Lakes and rare snow in the tropics. Meanwhile, the West has seen unusually warm temperatures.

Climate change has shrunk ice coverage, on average, year-to-year on Lake Superior, making ice-cave access anomalous. Eleven years ago, in 2015, I couldn’t make it to the caves, and I’ve had FOMO ever since.

The formation of 'Ice Flowers' is a natural phenomenon in the Great Lakes Region of North America When the temperature drops under freezing temperatures, the water under the ice layer is pushed upward, creating small cracks. Cont⬇️

Some fish species also use the ice for protection from predators during spawning season which relies on ice cover.. that cannot happen if the water is too warm #climate #cdnpoli #onpoli #LakeMichigan #GreenSky

Great Lakes ice is thought to block evaporation. A new study says weather plays a bigger role. Study challenges assumption that ice cover leads to reduced evaporation #GreatLakes www.wpr.org/news/great-l... agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10....

In the photo on the right, there are blocks of peat ripped up from an outcrop offshore. The peat predates the first ice advance into this area during the last glaciation (but I can't recall the 14C age someone got from the outcrop🤷‍♂️)
A couple years ago the wood pilings had ice caps that looked like bonnets or maybe jellyfish or plastic bags

" #GreatLakes ice coverage varies greatly from year to year, but trends show it declining since record-keeping started in the early 1970s. And a warming climate lends additional urgency to such efforts." .bsky.social grist.org/science/ice-...
Researchers want help from ice fishers to collect data on ice thickness to improve ice forecasting in the #GreatLakes. www.interlochenpublicradio.org/ipr-news/202...

Ice cover is WAY below average on the Great Lakes. The lake ice season acroos the N hemisphere is getting shorter because of climate change. The ice season is even shorter on the Great Lakes during El Nino winters, which tend to start mild.

I really thought ice cover would have had a substantial effect on evaporation from Lake Superior in winter. It would have some effect from first principles but apparently it is small. Here is a pic of steam fog evaporating from open water but impeded by ice during very cold winter conditions.
Ice coverage rests on the waters of the Great Lakes in various bays, inlets and along their coastlines while snow peppers the surrounding land, observed this afternoon by NOAA-21.
When the ice on Toronto Harbour starts to break up in late winter, Polar Bears who ventured onto the ice sheet to hunt seals start coming back to shore.

Winter in the northern hemisphere sees the growth of ice cover over bodies of water. In the Great Lakes, rounds of frigid Arctic air infiltrating the contiguous United States allowed ice cover to develop along the coast, in bays and inlets, and over Lake Erie.
No regrets on buying this mug seven years ago... it's still my daily driver!

There is still a lot of heat to be lost from the surface waters before we start to see any ice formation. You can see how the recent past influences this winter. Hoping for some cold #GreatLakes. www.glerl.noaa.gov/res/glcfs/

I'm not sure if I've seen it happening. Rime ice events in Toronto are often associated with temperatures between -4°C and -7°C and the presence of fog banks. The proximity to the Great Lakes can influence moisture levels, but the specific combination of fog and sub-zero temperatures is needed.

Funding for the NOAA Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research, including climate research, is preserved at a small bump over FY25 levels, instead of being zeroed out. The Trump plan to transfer weather programs to NWS is accepted.

Cloud coverage increases in the winter normally but with the changing climate and warmer water temperatures, it is accelerating evaporation on Lake Superior and Lake Michigan which in turn increases our cloud coverage.
the purple lines are break walls that trap ice and slow water flow. but you can see on the view from skydeck that ice is flowing out with the NE winds.
Ice cover on the Great Lakes varies annually depending on factors such as air temperature, wind, and lake depth. #geography
Also…for the Great Lakes: for the ice cover not just the annual maximum but maybe the average ice cover for the whole winter too and possibly also even lake water temperatures if that data exists?

Lake Ontario actually having below-average ice coverage for this time of year, hovering somewhere under 10% ice, is surprising given the cold we've been wacked with, but I guess all the precipitation has a way of discouraging ice growth?

Great Lakes average #ice cover drops to 6%, one of lowest levels ever recorded Scientists say global heating is driving ice loss and warmer water, as ice cover falls short of 50-year average of 18% www.theguardian.com/environment/... #GreatLakes #LakeSuperior #Yoopers #Michigan #MI #ClimateChange

Saw on the news some people were trying to walk out on the ice this weekend from Edgewater Beach and it just hasn’t been cold long enough yet.

I grew up on a bay in Lake Superior that used to freeze hard enough every winter to drive trucks on to cut blocks of ice, now it will barely freeze long enough to support a snowmobile.
Could the isolated extra warming over Lake Superior be due to shorter duration of ice coverage during January?

What a difference a week can make when it comes to ice cover on ice on Lake Superior! The entire coastline of western Lake Superior was analyzed to have some ice on it on yesterday’s analysis per the North American Ice Service/US National Ice Center. usicecenter.gov/Products/Gre...

Check out the Lake Superior Ice cover trends. In the 1970-1995 only 4 years had maximum ice extents of less than 60%, but that became commonplace in subsequent decades. The window for caves which call for 90% even fewer. www.nps.gov/articles/000...

No ice up there? We were watching an icebreaker on Lake Ontario yesterday, still a fair bit here. I tried using older Pentax lenses on my newer camera, I found the colour looked washed out compared to the cheap kit lens, yours doesn't...

Bonus: Denial that ice coverage has gone down wasn't mentioned, but that's important to us for some reason. So to be clear: Yes. It is. "During the winter period, average ice cover on the #GreatLakes declined 69 percent between 1973 and 2017" earthobservatory.nasa.gov/images/15097...

Our ice cube maker doesn't work in the freezer. So I'm happy to announce this winter I'll be traveling over 1000 miles back to Chicago to cut ice cubes out of Lake Michigan to provide for me and my family for 2026. Old traditions should be kept alive!
Current ice coverage + 72 hour ice coverage forecast. Ice is forecast to increase along the southern shore, including around Presque Isle.

I used Feb-Apr ONI≥0.4 as El Niño and ONI≤-0.4 as La Niña and cloud cover every 6 h from April 1-15. I expected more cloud cover over the south during El Niño due to a stronger subtropical jet. But the opposite seems true. There is some increased cloud cover over the lower Great Lakes, though. (2/2)

: The Great Lakes had the smallest amount of ice cover this New Year’s Day in at least the past 50 years and are on track to receive less than the seasonal average this winter, according to government data.

The Great Lakes are more affected by ice cover year to year. Last year was not real cold and some Lakes didn't freeze. Causes more evaporation which is why the Lakes go up and down so frequently. But less snowpack also means less runoff into the lakes. Melting polar caps fill oceans

Last year's winter was absolutely scorched, especially December and February! Especially the Great Lakes region that saw record low ice coverage. (anomalies relative to 1901-2000 mean) www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monit... www.climate.gov/news-feature...
After a much colder start to Winter this year, Great Lakes ice cover has grown quickly.
The U.S. Coast Guard has started its winter task of keeping newly formed ice from hindering commercial vessel traffic on the Great Lakes.
This doesn't apply for the East Coast, but for around the Great Lakes - diminishing ice cover as a direct result of climate change means more water available for lake effect snow
